zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Ellen de Button於大約1224年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾比頓時，她的父親Sir John de Button43歲，母親Lady Hawise Furneaux32歲。她於1238年與Sir John Joyce在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾可利阿維爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾可利阿維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Ellen de Button于大约1224年出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾比頓时，她的父亲Sir John de Button43岁，母亲Lady Hawise Furneaux32岁。她于1238年与Sir John Joyce在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾可利阿維爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾可利阿維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Button
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of buttons, from Old French bo(u)ton ‘knob, lump’, specialized to mean ‘button’. Compare Butner .
habitational name from Booton in Norfolk, named with either the Old English personal name Bōta or the Old Norse personal name Bō + tūn ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Booton
meanings: habitational name, probably from a place in Norfolk named Booton, from an Old English personal name (Bōta or Bō) + tūn ‘settlement’. The present-day concentration of the surname is in the West Midlands and Wales.

variants.name: Botten
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms named Botn, Botten, or Botnen, from Old Norse botn ‘small valley, valley end’. Compare Botner .

variants.name: Booten
meanings: English: variant of   Button  . Compare   Booton  .
In some cases possibly also Dutch and North German: patronymic from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name   Bodo  . This surname is rare in the Netherlands and Germany.

variants.name: Butner
meanings: English (Durham): occupational name of Norman origin from Old French botonier ‘button maker’, from bo(u)ton ‘knob, lump’, specialized to mean ‘button’.
In some cases also German (mainly Bütner): very rare variant of Büttner (see Buettner ) and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Ellen de Button was born about 1224, in Bitton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John de Button, was 43 and her mother, Lady Hawise Furneaux, was 32. She married Sir John Joyce in 1238, in Clearwell, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in Clearwell, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Ellen de Button est née environ 1224, à Bitton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John de Button, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Lady Hawise Furneaux, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Joyce en 1238, à Clearwell, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Clearwell, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Ellen de Button um 1224 in Bitton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Button 43 und ihre Mutter Lady Hawise Furneaux 32. Sie hat 1238 in Clearwell, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Joyce geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Clearwell, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Ellen de Button è nata il circa 1224 nel Bitton, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir John de Button, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Lady Hawise Furneaux, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Joyce nel 1238, nel Clearwell, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Clearwell, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Ellen de Buttonが約1224年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアビットオンで生まれた時，父Sir John de Buttonは43歳，母Lady Hawise Furneauxは32歳であった。1238年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアクレアルウエルでSir John Joyceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアクレアルウエルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Ellen de Button(이)가 대략 1224년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 비턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Button(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Hawise Furneaux(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1238년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 클리웰에서 Sir John Joyce(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 클리웰에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Ellen de Button nasceu aproximadamente 1224, em Bitton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John de Button, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Lady Hawise Furneaux, tinha 32 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Ellen de Button родилась около 1224 г. в местности биттон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir John de Button было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Hawise Furneaux, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Joyce в 1238 г. в местности клирвелл, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности клирвелл, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
